docs.title: Network Documentation Index
docs.text: About Me Contact Me

docs.title: Infrastructure
docs.text:
Info

The core infrastructure that makes up the LAN, such as: IP addressing, VLANs, VPNs, DDNS providers, etc.

  1. Disaster Recovery Plan: Ensure HA for critical network services and to provide a clear path to data restoration for stateful services in the event of hardware failure or data corruption.
  2. IP Address Management: Local network IPAM, VPN and VLAN information.
  3. MAC Address Tables: Tables containing devices on the LAN and their hardware MAC addresses.
  4. Network Security Policy: This infrastructure operates on the Principle of Least Privilege. No device or service is granted more network access than is strictly required for its primary function. Security is maintained through physical isolation, logical segmentation, and encrypted transit.
  5. Logical Map: Mermaid.js flowchart focusing on servers and services.
  6. Physical Map: Mermaid.js flowchart focusing on physical connections.

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
Info

The hardware on the LAN. (servers, clients, routers, switches, etc.)

  1. ASUS RT-BE92U: Main router for the local network.
  2. ASUS RT-AX55: Secondary router in the office upstairs, acting as an "AiMesh" node.
  3. Ben's Desktop: Ben's main desktop PC located in the office upstairs.
  4. Ben's Laptop: Ben's main laptop PC. Mobile device connected to the Trusted Wi-Fi network (SSID: Home).
  5. Debian Server VM: General purpose Debian Linux server hosted on ZimaBoard 2 NAS.
  6. Hitron Modem: DOCSIS 3.1 cable modem that communicates with the ISP (Xfinity).

  7. Kacey 3D-Printer: Creality K1C 3D-printer located in the office upstairs.

    More Creality K1C Info

    More detailed information about the Creality K1C hardware.

    More Kacey Info

  8. Raspberry Pi 4B Server: Main DNS server (Pi-hole), CUPS print server, Home Assistant server, and other Docker containers.

  9. Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W: Second DNS server (Pi-hole).
  10. Rob's Desktop: Rob's main desktop PC located in the office upstairs.
  11. TP-Link Switch: Core gigabit desktop switch (5-port).
  12. Ugreen Switch: Rack-mounted 2.5 gigabit switch in the living room.
  13. ZimaBoard 2 NAS: Primary NAS server & VM host.

docs.title: Services
docs.text:
Info

The applications and services hosted on the Local Area Network.

  1. Beszel Agent: Agent for Beszel Hub, a server utilization monitor.
  2. Beszel Hub: A self-hosted server utilization monitor.
  3. Caddy Caddy: Lightweight, open-source Web server written in Go. Used as a reverse-proxy for creating unique domains for locally hosted services.
  4. Cloudflare Cloudflared: A secure reverse-proxy tunnel for hosting private services. Currently, Immich and Uptime Kuma are utilizing this service.
  5. CUPS Print Server: Print server for USB-connected Brother_HL-L2300D_series laser printer.
  6. Dockge: A fancy, easy-to-use and reactive self-hosted Docker compose.yaml stack-oriented manager.
  7. F1 Replay Timing: Visualization of real-time track data and telemetry synced to F1 live and replays.
  8. Fluidd: A Web UI for managing the 3D-printer.
  9. Glance Glance: A server dashboard Web UI.
  10. Glances: A top / htop alternative for the Web browser.
  11. Home Assistant: A home automation and IoT dashboard.
  12. Homebox: An inventory and organization system built for the home user.
  13. Immich Immich: Photo gallery and backup service.
  14. IT-Tools: Handy tools for network administrators and developers.
  15. Moonraker: An API for the Klipper firmware.
  16. Nebula-Sync: Synchronize settings between Pi-hole instances.
  17. Nextcloud: Self-hosted cloud storage and collaboration platform.
  18. Network File System (NFS): Remote file system access.
  19. Nginx (Network Portfolio): The world's most popular Web Server, high performance Load Balancer, Reverse Proxy, API Gateway and Content Cache.
  20. ntopng: Network traffic probe.
  21. Obsidian LiveSync: A CouchDB database for synchronizing Obsidian Vaults.
  22. OpenSpeedTest: An HTML5 network speed test server.
  23. Pi-hole: A DNS server and ad/malware blocker.
  24. Portainer: A Docker container management platform.
  25. Portainer Agent: An agent for connecting remote servers to Portainer.
  26. Portracker: A self-hosted, real-time port monitoring and discovery tool.
  27. Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP): Remote desktop access over the local network. (not exposed to the internet)
  28. Server Message Block (SMB): Remote file system access.
  29. Spoolman: Manage 3D-printer filament inventory.
  30. Secure Shell (SSH): Remote shell access to servers
  31. Syncthing: Open decentralized file synchronization.
  32. Technitium: An open source authoritative as well as recursive DNS server that can be used for self hosting a DNS server for privacy & security.
  33. ttydBridge: Access the host terminal in a Web browser.
  34. Uptime Kuma: A self-hosted service monitoring tool.
  35. WireGuard Server: A lightweight, secure remote access VPN server.
  36. yt-dlp WebUI: A Web UI and RPC server for yt-dlp.

docs.title: Change Management
docs.text:
Info

Change management involves implementing approaches to prepare for organizational change.

  1. Caddy Caddy Reverse Proxy: Preparing the Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W to be a Caddy reverse proxy server to give unique domain names to services hosted on the local network.
  2. DNS Migration: Preparing to migrate from Pi-hole to Technitium for DNS queries on the local network.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
1. Creality Helper Script: Add features to your printer.
  2. Fluidd Docs: A lightweight & responsive user interface for Klipper.
  3. Klipper Docs: The Klipper firmware controls 3D-printers. It combines the power of a general purpose computer with one or more micro-controllers.
  4. Moonraker Docs: Moonraker is a Python 3 based web server that exposes APIs with which client applications may use to interact with the 3D printing firmware Klipper.

docs.title: Modifications
docs.text:
1. Bed Leveling Knobs: Installing a bed leveling modification on the Creality K1C.
  2. Manual Nozzle Cleaning G-Code Macro: Enabling a custom g-code macro I wrote for manually cleaning the nozzle with a silicone brush.
  3. PROWIPER© Mod: Installing the PROWIPER© Mod, and editing the required g-code on the Creality K1C.

docs.title: Linux Tutorials
docs.text:
Info

Documenting the Linux tutorials I have written. Check out these tutorials in this GitHub repository:

Linux Tutorials

  1. Defining Terminal Aliases: How to define Bash terminal aliases in their own file to avoid a cluttered .bashrc file. The methodology is different on Debian and RHEL / Fedora based Linux distributions.

    My Terminal Aliases

    Here is an aggregated list of Bash terminal aliases that I find useful in my daily workflow.

    My Aliases

  2. Immich Immich Slideshow for Nest Hub: How to replace the Google Photos slideshow on the Nest Hub with an Immich slideshow utilizing an immich-frame container and Home Assistant with Google Cast.

  3. Setup SSH Login Email Notification: How to set up an email notification delivered to your inbox every time a new SSH session is established; utilizing a Bash script, msmtp and pam_exec.so.

  4. Unlocking LUKS2 Volumes with TPM2: How to unlock your encrypted LUKS2 volumes with the TPM2 when the system boots.

    Update PCRs Script

    In this GitHub repository I have written a Bash script, update-pcrs, that automates the process of clearing and registering new PCRs and regenerating the initramfs after a firmware or kernel upgrade. The script is full-featured with flags for using custom PCRs (defaults to 0+4+7+11), usage help, checking the version, and defining the device path.

    Update PCRs

docs.title: About Me & Professional Philosophy
docs.text: My Resume Contact Me

docs.title: From Engines to Infrastructure
docs.text:
With 13 years of experience as an Automotive Technician and Maryland State Inspector, I spent over a decade diagnosing complex mechanical and electrical systems. In 2022, I pivoted that diagnostic mindset toward Information Technology and Cybersecurity.

I’ve found that whether it’s a fuel injection system or a network stack, the core principles remain the same: precision, security, and relentless troubleshooting.

docs.title: My Technical North Star
docs.text:
I am a firm believer in the Self-Hosted and Open-Source movement. My home lab isn’t just a hobby; it’s a sandbox for testing the "Defense in Depth" strategies I’m studying for my IT / Cybersecurity career.

I prioritize:

  • Privacy-First Networking: Using tools like Technitium DNS and Network Attached Storage to take back control of my data.
  • Immutable Documentation: Maintaining a "Single Source of Truth" using Markdown and Obsidian to ensure the infrastructure is reproducible and transparent.
  • Continuous Learning: From earning my CompTIA A+ & Linux+ to mastering Docker orchestration, I am always looking for the next "bottleneck" to solve.

docs.title: Beyond the Terminal
docs.text:
When I’m not hardening my network or managing services, you can usually find me:

  • 3D Printing: Tinkering with my Creality K1C (and likely troubleshooting a new filament).
  • At the Track: Following Formula 1 and other motorsports—old habits from the automotive days die hard.
  • In the Sim: Competing in competitive online sim-racing league races.
  • Unplugging: Spending time outdoors and / or hanging out with my husband and our dog, Max.

I’m currently looking for opportunities where I can apply my unique blend of hands-on mechanical diagnostic experience and modern IT security principles to solve real-world infrastructure challenges.

docs.title: Disaster Recovery Plan
docs.text:
Objective

The goal of this DR plan is to ensure HA for critical network services (DNS, Routing, Security) and to provide a clear path to data restoration for stateful services (Immich, Home Assistant, SMB Shares) in the event of hardware failure or data corruption.

docs.title: Service Priority Levels
docs.text: Priority Service Impact of Failure Recovery Time Objective (RTO) Critical Technitium DNS Cluster Total loss of internet/LAN connectivity. < 5 Minutes (Automatic Failover) High Caddy Reverse Proxy Loss of remote access and SSL termination. < 1 Hour Medium Home Assistant / IoT Loss of automation and physical device control. < 4 Hours Standard Immich / Media Loss of access to personal archives. < 24 Hours

docs.title: Backup Strategy
docs.text:
Info

To prevent data loss, the "3-2-1" backup rule is applied across the infrastructure.

docs.title: On-Site Synchronization:
docs.text:
• Syncthing is utilized across the Raspberry Pi 4B, Raspberry Pi Zero, Debian Server and ZimaOS NAS nodes to ensure configuration files and Docker volumes are mirrored in real-time.
  • Linux PCs, ben-workstation and ben-laptop, have the /home directory backed up to the ZimaOS NAS every weekday at 18:00 and 19:00 UTC-5 using a custom script, home-bkp-nas.sh, that utilizes NFS and rsync.
  • Windows PCs use SMB and the built-in backup utility in Windows 11 to back up personal data to the ZimaOS NAS.
  • Obsidian vaults are synchronized in real-time with a CouchDB database hosted on the ZimaOS NAS, and to a private GitHub repository.
  • Android smartphones have SMS/MMS & RCS messages and critical application configurations backed up while charging to the ZimaOS NAS via Syncthing. The backup archives for messages are created locally on the device with the SMS Backup & Restore Pro application.

docs.title: Cold Storage:
docs.text:
• Critical database dumps (Home Assistant, Immich PostgreSQL) are exported weekly to the USB Storage (32GB) attached to the Raspberry Pi 4B server and the NVMe Storage (500GB) on the ZimaOS NAS.
  • The configuration of the ASUS RT-BE92U wireless router is exported weekly (Sundays @ 1:00 UTC-5) to the USB Storage (32GB) attached to the Raspberry Pi 4B server and the Quick-Storage (2TB) on the ZimaOS NAS via the backupmon utility included with the Asuswrt-merlin firmware.

docs.title: Off-Site / Cloud:
docs.text: • Encrypted backups of the Obsidian vaults and key configuration files are synced to a secure cloud provider (p-Cloud).

docs.title: Node Failure: Primary DNS
docs.text:
• Automated Failover:
    • The Technitium Secondary DNS (Raspberry Pi 4B) is configured as a secondary resolver in the ASUS RT-BE92U DHCP settings. Clients will automatically fallback to the Pi 4B.
  • Reprovisioning:
    • If the Debian VM or ZimaOS host is lost, the Debian VM is restored from the last known good snapshot stored on the NFS Share.

docs.title: Node Failure: Reverse Proxy
docs.text:
• Traffic Rerouting:
    • Since the Pi Zero 2W handles external entries via Caddy, a failure here breaks external URLs.
  • Intervention:
    • Deploy a temporary Caddy Docker container on the ZimaOS NAS using the synced configuration files from Syncthing.
  • Update DNS:
    • Point the Cloudflare tunnel or local DNS records to the ZimaOS NAS IP.

docs.title: Testing & Maintenance
docs.text:
• Quarterly "Pull the Plug" Test:
    • Physically disconnect the ZimaOS NAS to verify the Raspberry Pi 4B picks up all DNS traffic without user intervention.
  • Backup Verification:
    • Monthly check of the Immich library integrity and a trial restoration of a single Docker container from Syncthing data.
    • Weekly check of client backup logs for backup errors.
  • Monitoring:
    • Server status and service uptime is monitored by Beszel Hub and Uptime Kuma instances.
    • Email notifications are enabled on both instances. Notifications are sent when a server has an issue or a service is inaccessible
    • The ASUS router utilizing the Asuswrt-merlin firmware also has email notifications enabled for firmware / script updates, critical firewall messages, WAN connection quality degradation / dropouts, and router reboots.
    • The email address, admin@haube-pereira.com, is dedicated to automated notifications from the monitoring software.

            HA("Home Assistant"):::service
            Print("CUPS Print Server"):::service
            Syncthing_Pi4("Syncthing"):::service
            Service_Docker_Pi4("<b>Docker Containers:</b><br>Beszel Agent<br>Beszel Hub<br>Dockge<br>F1 API<br>Glance<br>Glances<br>IT-Tools<br>Portracker<br>Uptime Kuma"):::service
        end

        %% --- Pi 4B storage connections --- %%
        DNS_Secondary -- "Storage" --> MicroSD_Pi4
        HA -- "Storage" --> MicroSD_Pi4
        Service_Docker_Pi4 -- "Storage" --> MicroSD_Pi4
        Print -- "Storage" --> MicroSD_Pi4
        Syncthing_Pi4 -- "Storage" --> MicroSD_Pi4
        Server_Pi4 -. "USB Storage" .- USB_Ext

        %% --- Raspberry Pi Zero Server --- %%
        subgraph Server_PiZero ["<b>Pi Zero 2 W</b> <i>(.3)</i>"]
            MicroSD_PiZero[("<b>MicroSD Storage</b><br><i>(Debian Trixie)</i>")]:::storage
            Service_Docker_PiZero("<b>Docker Containers:</b><br>Beszel Agent<br>Dockge"):::service
            Syncthing_PiZero("Syncthing"):::service
            Caddy("<b>Caddy Reverse Proxy</b><br><i>(Unique domain names for local services)</i>"):::service
        end

        %% --- Pi Zero storage connections --- %%
        Service_Docker_PiZero -- "Storage" --> MicroSD_PiZero
        Caddy -- "Storage" --> MicroSD_PiZero
        Syncthing_PiZero -- "Storage" --> MicroSD_PiZero

    end

    %% --- Segregated VLANs --- %%
    subgraph IoT_VLAN53 ["<b>VLAN 53: IoT Network</b> <i>(192.168.53.x)</i>"]
        direction TB
        IoTClients("IoT Clients"):::vlan53  
    end

    subgraph Guest_VLAN52 ["<b>VLAN 52: Guest Wi-Fi</b> <i>(192.168.52.x)</i>"]
        direction TB
        GuestClients("Guest Clients"):::vlan52
    end

    %% --- Trusted VLAN Connections --- %%
    Clients -- "DNS Query" --> DNS_Primary
    Clients -- "DNS Query" --> DNS_Secondary
    Clients -- "HTTPS" --> Caddy
    Caddy -- "DNS Query" --> DNS_Primary
    Caddy -- "DNS Query" --> DNS_Secondary
    HA -. "Device Control<br><i>(wlan0)</i>" .- IoT_VLAN53
    Clients -- "SMB / NFS / Backups" --> Storage_SMB
    Clients -- "Photo Backup<br><i>(Immich)</i>" --> Storage_SMB
    Clients -- "Manage" --> HA
    Clients -- "Manage" --> Printer
    Clients -- "Print" --> Print
    Printer -- "Status / Video" --> HA
    Print -. "USB Printer" .- Brother

    %% --- Router --> VLAN Connections --- %%
    Router -- "VLAN50" --> Trusted_VLAN50
    Router -- "VLAN52" --> Guest_VLAN52
    Router -- "VLAN53" --> IoT_VLAN53
    AiMesh_Node -- "VLAN50" --> Trusted_VLAN50

docs.title: Network Security Policy (NSP)
docs.text:
Security Philosophy

This infrastructure operates on the Principle of Least Privilege. No device or service is granted more network access than is strictly required for its primary function. Security is maintained through physical isolation, logical segmentation, and encrypted transit.

docs.title: Network Segmentation (VLAN Architecture)
docs.text:
Info

The network is divided into three distinct zones to minimize the attack surface.

Zone VLAN ID Description Access Rules Trusted vlan50 Primary workstations, mobile devices, and core servers Full access to all internal zones; restricted outbound Guest vlan52 Temporary visitor devices Internet access only; strictly isolated from all other VLANs IoT vlan53 Smart home hardware (bulbs, sensors, etc.) No Internet Access by default; communication only with Home Assistant

docs.title: Boundary Defense & Remote Access
docs.text:
• Edge Protection:
    • An ASUS RT-BE92U acts as the primary firewall, utilizing SPI and DoS protection.
  • External Access:
    • Direct port forwarding is strictly prohibited.
    • Remote access to individual services is facilitated through a Cloudflare Tunnel, ensuring all incoming traffic is proxied and SSL-encrypted.
  • VPN:
    • For administrative tasks, there are two WireGuard instances providing a secure, encrypted tunnel into the Trusted zone.
    • The primary WireGuard server is hosted natively on the main ASUS router.
    • The secondary / backup WireGuard server is hosted in a Docker container on the ZimaOS NAS.

docs.title: DNS Security & Content Filtering
docs.text:
• Internal Resolution:
    • A clustered Technitium DNS environment (Debian VM & Pi 4B Server) handles all local queries.
  • Upstream Privacy:
    • Queries are forwarded to Quad-9 via DoT to prevent ISP snooping and MITM (Man-in-the-Middle) attacks.
  • Blocking:
    • Network-wide ad and malware blocking is enforced at the DNS level using curated blocklists to neutralize telemetry and malicious domains.

docs.title: Device & Host Hardening
docs.text:
• Operating Systems:
    • Servers primarily utilize Debian and ZimaOS (on the NAS) for stability and security.
      • Debian Linux (Stable) is a rock-solid, unchanging base to build a server on. Packages are not frequently updated with new features, so there is no need to worry about breakages. The 10-year-long service life means you are guaranteed to get security updates for the server's lifetime.
      • The immutability of ZimaOS means OS updates on the NAS are easy to revert if they cause a breakage, and ensures no system files can be modified by an intruder.
      • Both of these server operating systems ensure maximum security and availability for services hosted on the network.
    • Client PCs utilize Fedora (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) and Windows 11 for their frequent security updates, up-to-date packages with the latest features, and support for the newest hardware (e.g., the latest CPUs & GPUs).
    • Mobile devices (smartphones and tablets) utilize Android. Only Google Pixel and Samsung Galaxy devices are allowed due to their monthly security patches and long service & support lifespan.
  • Access Control:
    • SSH access requires Ed25519 Key-Based Authentication; password-based SSH is disabled.
    • Bitwarden is utilized to securely store passwords, API keys, and SSH keys.
      • Bitwarden's desktop app has a built-in SSH key-agent; allowing private keys to be stored securely.
      • The Chromium and Firefox browser extensions auto-fill passwords and integrate with the desktop app; allowing the use of biometric authentication.
      • The mobile application auto-fills passwords on websites and native applications.
    • Local user accounts on the Fedora and Windows 11 PCs do not have administrator privileges. A separate "admin" account is required to make system-level changes.
    • Services hosted on the network that require login have separate "admin" accounts for administration. The regular user accounts have reduced permissions to increase security.
  • Updates:
    • Automated security patching is enabled for critical packages on the Debian servers with unattended-upgrades.
    • Weekly manual audits for Docker container image updates (monitored via Dockge).
    • Desktop and Laptop PCs have automatic updates enabled.
  • 3D Infrastructure:
    • The Creality K1C is isolated to prevent unauthorized control while remaining accessible to the Trusted zone for print management.

docs.title: IoT & Smart Home Integrity
docs.text:
• The migration from Google Home to Home Assistant ensures that IoT devices are managed locally.
  • By isolating VLAN 53, "phone-home" telemetry from generic IoT hardware is neutralized, preventing potential lateral movement if a device is compromised.

docs.title: Maintenance & Notes
docs.text:
Critical Configurations

  1. Chrony:

    • Time synchronizes with local time server running on main router, ASUS RT-BE92U, using chrony.

  2. Backup:

    • Home directory backed up every weekday at 18:00 using custom rsync script, home-bkp-nas.sh. The script is triggered by systemd user timer, home-bkp-nas.timer which starts home-bkp-nas.service. The timer is persistent, so if the PC is asleep at the backup time the backup will start the next time the PC wakes up.

  3. LUKS Unlocking:

    • The LUKS encrypted boot drive is configured to use the onboard TPM2 chip for drive encryption. Kernel and firmware upgrades require clearing the registered PCRs and enrolling the new ones. This can be done with a custom script I wrote, update-pcrs, which is located in the /usr/local/bin directory.

    • The proper command format is: 

      sudo update-pcrs /dev/nvme0n1p3

    • More information about the configuration process can be found at: Unlock LUKS w/ TPM2

• sudo dnf upgrade -y --offline

docs.title: Backup Policy:
docs.text: • /home directory backed up every weekday at 18:00 to ZimaBoard 2 NAS

docs.title: Backup Script Config
docs.text:
1. Place home-bkp-nas.sh in the ~/.local/bin directory.
home-bkp-nas.sh
#!/bin/bash

# Backup the /home/ directory to the ZimaOS NAS
# GOAL: Pure data backup (No symlinks, no ownership retention)
# Exclusions defined in: /home/bhaube/.bkp-exclude-nas

# --- Configuration ---
SOURCE_DIR="$HOME/"
DEST_DIR="/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage/Backup/bens-workstation/"
MOUNT_POINT="/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage"
EXCLUDE_FILE="$HOME/.bkp-exclude-nas"
LOG_FILE="$HOME/.var/log/backup_log.log"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

# --- Environment Validation ---
# Ensure log rotation is configured to prevent infinite log growth
LOGROTATE_CONF="/etc/logrotate.d/home-backup"
if [ ! -f "$LOGROTATE_CONF" ]; then
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] WARNING: Logrotate config not found at $LOGROTATE_CONF." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Logs will grow indefinitely until this is fixed." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
fi

# --- Safety Check: Trigger Automount & Verify ---
# Access the mount point to wake up systemd automount
ls "$MOUNT_POINT" > /dev/null 2>&1

# Check if it is actually a mount point
if ! mountpoint -q "$MOUNT_POINT"; then
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] CRITICAL: NAS is not mounted at $MOUNT_POINT. Backup aborted." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the sub-directory exists on the NAS
mkdir -p "$DEST_DIR"

# Print status to terminal & log
echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Starting home directory backup..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# --- The Rsync Command ---
# -av: Archive mode + increase verbosity
# --progress: Show progress during transfer
# --timeout=60: Sets IO timeout to 60s
# --delete: Remove files on NAS that were deleted from Source
rsync -av --progress --timeout=60 --delete --exclude-from="$EXCLUDE_FILE" "$SOURCE_DIR" "$DEST_DIR" >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1

# Capture the exit code immediately
EXIT_CODE=$?

# Update the TIMESTAMP variable
TIMESTAMP=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

# Report Status based on captured code
if [ $EXIT_CODE -eq 0 ]; then
    MSG="[$TIMESTAMP] Backup completed successfully."
else
    MSG="[$TIMESTAMP] Backup **FAILED** with exit code $EXIT_CODE."
fi

# Print to terminal and append to log
echo "$MSG"
echo "$MSG" >> "$LOG_FILE"
  1. Place .bkp-exclude-nas in the ~/ directory.
.bkp-exclude-nas

  1. Run the command mkdir -p ~/.var/log to create the log directory.
  2. Run the command touch ~/.var/log/backup_log.log to create the empty log file.
  3. Place home-backup in the /etc/logrotate.d/ directory.
/etc/logrotate.d/home-backup
/home/bhaube/.var/log/backup_log.log {
    weekly
    rotate 4
    compress
    delaycompress
    missingok
    notifempty
    copytruncate
}
  1. Place home-bkp-nas.timer and home-bkp-nas.service in the ~/.config/systemd/user/ directory.
home-bkp-nas.timer
[Unit]
Description=Run NAS Backup Weekdays at 6PM

[Timer]
# Mon-Fri at 18:00 (6 PM)
OnCalendar=Mon..Fri 18:00:00
# If the computer is off/asleep at 6pm, run immediately when it wakes up
Persistent=true
# Randomize delay by 5 mins to prevent exact-second spikes (optional but good practice)
RandomizedDelaySec=5m

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
home-bkp-nas.service
[Unit]
Description=Daily NAS Backup for Home Directory
# Verify network is up before starting (checks the system mount)
ConditionPathIsMountPoint=/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage

[Service]
Type=oneshot
# Adjust this path if you saved the script somewhere else
ExecStart=/home/bhaube/.local/bin/home-bkp-nas.sh
# Nice behavior: runs with lower CPU/IO priority so it doesn't slow down your PC
Nice=19
IOSchedulingClass=idle

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
  1. Run the command systemctl --user daemon-reload
  2. Run the command systemctl --user enable home-bkp-nas.timer

docs.title: Systemd Files for ZimaOS NAS Mounts (NFS)
docs.text:
mnt-storage_server-NVMe.mount
[Unit]  
Description=Mount ZimaOS Quick-Storage  
After=network-online.target  
Wants=network-online.target  

[Mount]  
# The remote share  
What=192.168.50.4:/media/nvme0n1p1  
# The local path  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/NVMe  
Type=nfs  
# Standard NFS options for performance  
Options=defaults,noatime,nodiratime,rsize=1048576,wsize=1048576,timeo=14,soft  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target
mnt-storage_server-NVMe.automount
[Unit]  
Description=Automount for ZimaOS Quick-Storage  

[Automount]  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/NVMe  
# Unmount if idle for 10 minutes (optional, saves resources)  
TimeoutIdleSec=600  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target
mnt-storage_server-Quick_Storage.mount
[Unit]  
Description=Mount ZimaOS Quick-Storage via NFS  
After=network-online.target  
Wants=network-online.target  

[Mount]  
# The remote share  
What=192.168.50.4:/media/Quick-Storage  
# The local path  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage  
Type=nfs  
# Standard NFS options for performance  
Options=defaults,noatime,nodiratime,rsize=1048576,wsize=1048576,timeo=14,soft  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target
mnt-storage_server-Quick_Storage.automount
[Unit]  
Description=Automount for ZimaOS NAS Quick-Storage  

[Automount]  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage  
# Unmount if idle for 10 minutes (optional, saves resources)  
TimeoutIdleSec=600  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Type=Application

docs.title: Starship.rs Terminal Prompt
docs.text:
1. Install the latest version.
curl -sS https://starship.rs/install.sh | sh
  1. Add init script to shell's config file. 
eval "$(starship init bash)"
  1. Move custom config file to ~/.config/starship.toml.
starship.toml
# Get editor completions based on the config schema
"$schema" = 'https://starship.rs/config-schema.json'

# Inserts a blank line between shell prompts
add_newline = true

# Move the directory to the second line
# format = '$all$directory$character'

# move the rest of the prompt to the right
# right_format = """$all"""

continuation_prompt = '[❯❯ ](bright-black)'

[character]
success_symbol = '[❯](bold green)'
error_symbol = '[❯](bold red)'

[username]
style_user = 'bold white'
style_root = 'bold red'
format = '[$user]($style) '
disabled = false
show_always = true

[hostname]
ssh_only = false
ssh_symbol = " "
format = '@ [$ssh_symbol$hostname](bold cyan) '
disabled = false

[directory]
style = 'bold white'
truncation_length = 8
truncation_symbol = '…/'
read_only = " 󰌾"
read_only_style = 'bold red'

[localip]
ssh_only = true
format = '- [$localipv4](bold white) '
disabled = false

[os]
format = "on [($name) $symbol]($style)"
style = "bold cyan"
disabled = false

[cmd_duration]
style = 'bold cyan'
format = 'took [$duration]($style) '

#!/bin/bash

MOUNT_POINT="/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage"
EXCLUDE_FILE="$HOME/.bkp-exclude-nas"
LOG_FILE="$HOME/.var/log/backup_log.log"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

# --- Environment Validation ---
# Ensure log rotation is configured to prevent infinite log growth
LOGROTATE_CONF="/etc/logrotate.d/home-backup"
if [ ! -f "$LOGROTATE_CONF" ]; then
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] WARNING: Logrotate config not found at $LOGROTATE_CONF." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Logs will grow indefinitely until this is fixed." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# --- Safety Check: Trigger Automount & Verify ---
# Access the mount point to wake up systemd automount

# Check if it is actually a mount point
if ! mountpoint -q "$MOUNT_POINT"; then

    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the sub-directory exists on the NAS
mkdir -p "$DEST_DIR"

# Print status to terminal & log
echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Starting home directory backup..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# --- The Rsync Command ---
# -av: Archive mode + increase verbosity
# --progress: Show progress during transfer
# --timeout=60: Sets IO timeout to 60s
# --delete: Remove files on NAS that were deleted from Source
rsync -av --progress --timeout=60 --delete --exclude-from="$EXCLUDE_FILE" "$SOURCE_DIR" "$DEST_DIR" >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1

# Capture the exit code immediately

# Update the TIMESTAMP variable

# Report Status based on captured code

    MSG="[$TIMESTAMP] Backup completed successfully."

    MSG="[$TIMESTAMP] Backup **FAILED** with exit code $EXIT_CODE."

  1. Run the command mkdir -p ~/.var/log to create the log directory.
  2. Run the command touch ~/.var/log/backup_log.log to create the empty log file.
  3. Place home-backup in the /etc/logrotate.d/ directory.
/etc/logrotate.d/home-backup
/home/bhaube/.var/log/backup_log.log {
    weekly
    rotate 4
    compress
    delaycompress
    missingok
    notifempty
    copytruncate
}
  1. Place home-bkp-nas.timer and home-bkp-nas.service in the ~/.config/systemd/user/ directory.
home-bkp-nas.timer
[Unit]
Description=Run NAS Backup Weekdays at 7PM

[Timer]
# Mon-Fri at 19:00 (7 PM)
OnCalendar=Mon..Fri 19:00:00
# If the computer is off/asleep at 6pm, run immediately when it wakes up
Persistent=true
# Randomize delay by 5 mins to prevent exact-second spikes (optional but good practice)
RandomizedDelaySec=5m

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
home-bkp-nas.service
[Unit]
Description=Daily NAS Backup for Home Directory
# Verify network is up before starting (checks the system mount)
ConditionPathIsMountPoint=/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage

[Service]
Type=oneshot
# Adjust this path if you saved the script somewhere else
ExecStart=/home/bhaube/.local/bin/home-bkp-nas.sh
# Nice behavior: runs with lower CPU/IO priority so it doesn't slow down your PC
Nice=19
IOSchedulingClass=idle

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
  1. Run the command systemctl --user daemon-reload
  2. Run the command systemctl --user enable home-bkp-nas.timer

docs.title: Systemd Files for ZimaOS NAS Mounts (NFS)
docs.text:
mnt-storage_server-NVMe.mount
[Unit]  
Description=Mount ZimaOS Quick-Storage  
After=network-online.target  
Wants=network-online.target  

[Mount]  
# The remote share  
What=192.168.50.4:/media/nvme0n1p1  
# The local path  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/NVMe  
Type=nfs  
# Standard NFS options for performance  
Options=defaults,noatime,nodiratime,rsize=1048576,wsize=1048576,timeo=14,soft  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target
mnt-storage_server-NVMe.automount
[Unit]  
Description=Automount for ZimaOS Quick-Storage  

[Automount]  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/NVMe  
# Unmount if idle for 10 minutes (optional, saves resources)  
TimeoutIdleSec=600  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target
mnt-storage_server-Quick_Storage.mount
[Unit]  
Description=Mount ZimaOS Quick-Storage via NFS  
After=network-online.target  
Wants=network-online.target  

[Mount]  
# The remote share  
What=192.168.50.4:/media/Quick-Storage  
# The local path  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage  
Type=nfs  
# Standard NFS options for performance  
Options=defaults,noatime,nodiratime,rsize=1048576,wsize=1048576,timeo=14,soft  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target
mnt-storage_server-Quick_Storage.automount
[Unit]  
Description=Automount for ZimaOS NAS Quick-Storage  

[Automount]  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage  
# Unmount if idle for 10 minutes (optional, saves resources)  
TimeoutIdleSec=600  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target

docs.title: Encrypted-Documents Config
docs.text:
1. Open kdewallet, create a folder named Passwords, create an entry called gocryptfspass, and type in the password.
  2. Place the .desktop file in the ~/.config/autostart directory.
mount-gocryptfs.desktop
[Desktop Entry]
Exec=gocryptfs --extpass="kwallet-query -f Passwords -r gocryptfspass kdewallet" ~/.cipher ~/Encrypted-Documents
Name=mountgocryptfs
Icon=plasmavault
Type=Application

docs.title: Rclone Google Drive Config
docs.text:
1. Place therclone.conf file in the ~/.config/rclone directory.
rclone.conf
[Google-Drive]
type = drive
client_id = <id>.apps.googleusercontent.com
client_secret = <secret>
scope = drive
token = {token}
auth_url = https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth
token_url = https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
metadata_owner = failok
metadata_permissions = failok
metadata_labels = failok
env_auth = false
description = rclone
team_drive =
  1. Place the .desktop file in the ~/.config/autostart directory.
mount-rclone.desktop
[Desktop Entry]
Type=Application
Name=Mount Google Drive
Exec=rclone mount Google-Drive: ~/Google-Drive --vfs-cache-mode full --vfs-cache-max-size 10G --vfs-read-chunk-size 32M --daemon
Icon=google-drive
Comment=Mount Google Drive via rclone

docs.title: Starship.rs Terminal Prompt
docs.text:
1. Install the latest version.
curl -sS https://starship.rs/install.sh | sh
  1. Add init script to shell's config file. 
eval "$(starship init bash)"
  1. Move custom config file to ~/.config/starship.toml.
starship.toml
# Get editor completions based on the config schema
"$schema" = 'https://starship.rs/config-schema.json'

# Inserts a blank line between shell prompts
add_newline = true

# Move the directory to the second line
# format = '$all$directory$character'

# move the rest of the prompt to the right
# right_format = """$all"""

continuation_prompt = '[❯❯ ](bright-black)'

[character]
success_symbol = '[❯](bold green)'
error_symbol = '[❯](bold red)'

[battery]
format = '[$symbol$percentage ]($style)'
full_symbol = '󰁹 '
charging_symbol = '󰂄 '
discharging_symbol = '󰂃 '
empty_symbol = '󰂎 '

[[battery.display]]
threshold = 10
style = 'bold red'
charging_symbol = '󰢜 '
discharging_symbol = '󱃍 '

docs.title: Debian Server VM
docs.text: Debian Debian Docs

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Role: Primary DNS server (Technitium Cluster)
  • Hostname(s): debian-vm
  • Hypervisor: ZimaOS (KVM / QEMU)
  • Operating System:
    • Debian Linux 13 (Trixie)
  • Host Node:
    • ZimaBoard 2 NAS
  • Credentials:
    • Bitwarden Bitwarden SSH key: "server-admin@debian-vm"

docs.title: Resource Allocation
docs.text:
• vCPU: 2 Cores
  • RAM: 2 GB
  • Storage: 50GB Virtual Disk Image
    • Disk Location on Host: /media/nvme0n1p1/VM/debian-vm/cd175b11

docs.title: Network Interface:
docs.text: Interface IP Address MAC Address Notes enp1s0 192.168.50.6 52:54:00:28:BB:EA Bridged Adapter (Appears as separate device on LAN)

docs.title: IP Configuration:
docs.text: VLAN Hostname(s) DNS Servers Gateway vlan50 debian-vm.internal 127.0.0.1 192.168.50.1

docs.title: Native Linux:
docs.text: Status Service Port(s) Role / Notes Active SSH (Secure Shell) 22 Remote terminal access. Active Technitium 53 443 5380 An open source authoritative as well as recursive DNS server that can be used for self hosting a DNS server for privacy & security.

docs.title: Docker:
docs.text: Status Service Port(s) Role / Notes Active Beszel Agent 45876 Agent for Beszel Hub (hosted on Raspberry Pi 4B Server). Active Dockge 5001 A fancy, easy-to-use and reactive self-hosted Docker compose.yaml stack-oriented manager.

docs.title: Maintenance & Snapshots
Update Policy:
docs.text:
• sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade (Standard Debian)
  • unattended-upgrades enabled for critical bug and security fixes.

docs.title: Snapshot Policy:
docs.text:
• Perform external snapshots before major changes.

Warning!

  1. The KVM / QEMU hypervisor on ZimaOS only supports external snapshots.

  2. To create an external snapshot, run the following command as root: 

    virsh snapshot-create-as <vm_name> <snapshot-name> "External snapshot description" --disk-only --atomic

docs.title: Recovery:
docs.text:
• If the VM is corrupted there are external snapshots to roll back to.
  • Also, on the main storage pool on the ZimaBoard 2 NAS in the /media/Quick_Storage/Backup/virsh-backups directory there is a backup disk image and XML settings file.

docs.title: Maintenance & Notes
docs.text: Modifications:

docs.title: Firmware:
docs.text: • The standard firmware from Creality is heavily modified with the Creality Helper Script. See documentation for configuration issues.

docs.title: Software:
docs.text:
• Fluidd / Moonraker
    • For information regarding the Fluidd Web UI see the documentation.
    • Moonraker is an API that allows Fluidd to communicate with Klipper. See Moonraker documentation.
  • Klipper
    • For information regarding Klipper configuration see the documentation.

docs.title: Hardware:
docs.text:
•  Bed Leveling Knobs

    • Changes from a fixed bed to an adjustable bed with aluminum knobs.
    • See the tutorial for information about how to install and use the Screws_Tilt_Adjust gcode macro.

  •  PROWIPER© Mod

    • Replaces the standard nozzle wiping brush at the back of the build plate.
    • See the tutorial for information on how to install and activate the required G-Code macros.

Warning

Add these coordinates to "Bed Exclude Area" in OrcaSlicer printer settings to avoid collisions during prints: 

70x210, 150x210, 150x220, 70x220

docs.title: Custom G-Code Macros:
docs.text:
• Manual Nozzle Cleaning Macro
    • This custom macro set (CLEAN_NOZZLE, DONE_CLEANING, and DONE_CLEANING_COOL) creates an interactive, semi-automated workflow for manual nozzle maintenance.
    • See more information and G-Code here.

docs.title: Update Process:
docs.text:
• Update most software through the Fluidd Web UI.
  • Update Entware packages in terminal via SSH

docs.title: Backup Policy:
docs.text: • Configuration files are backed up automatically to a private GitHub repository.

docs.title: Maintenance & Notes
docs.text:
Immutable OS

  1. This device runs ZimaOS. Do not attempt to use apt to install system packages or change NTP settings (stuck on systemd-timesyncd).
  2. The Entware package manager is installed. Additional packages can be installed to the /opt/bin directory with the opkg command. Check the Entware Wiki for documentation.

SMB Configuration

SMB Multi-channel is enabled via custom edit to /etc/samba/smb.conf (or ZimaOS equivalent path).

  • Constraint: Requires both Ethernet cables to be connected to the 2.5Gb switch to achieve throughput > 2.5Gbps.
NAS Protocol Change (SMB → NFS)

Date: 2026-02-09 Reason: SMB latency caused slow transfers for small files. (e.g., photos / code) Affected Clients:

  • Ben's Desktop
  • Ben's Laptop

Performance: Reduced backup time from ~10m to ~1.5m.

docs.title: Server Side (ZimaOS):
docs.text:
• Config File: etc/exports

  • Exports Entry:

    /etc/exports
    /media/Quick-Storage *(rw,sync,no_subtree_check)  
/media/nvme0n1p1 *(rw,sync,no_subtree_check)

  • Command to apply:

    exportfs -ra

    or

    systemctl restart nfs-server

docs.title: Client Side (Fedora 43):
docs.text:
• Package required: nfs-utils

  • Systemd .mount unit files: /etc/systemd/system

    mnt-storage_server-Quick_Storage.mount
    [Unit]  
Description=Mount ZimaOS Quick-Storage via NFS  
After=network-online.target  
Wants=network-online.target  

[Mount]  
# The remote share  
What=192.168.50.4:/media/Quick-Storage  
# The local path  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage  
Type=nfs  
# Standard NFS options for performance  
Options=defaults,noatime,nodiratime,rsize=1048576,wsize=1048576,timeo=14,soft  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target
    mnt-storage_server-NVMe.mount
    [Unit]  
Description=Mount ZimaOS Quick-Storage  
After=network-online.target  
Wants=network-online.target  

[Mount]  
# The remote share  
What=192.168.50.4:/media/nvme0n1p1  
# The local path  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/NVMe  
Type=nfs  
# Standard NFS options for performance  
Options=defaults,noatime,nodiratime,rsize=1048576,wsize=1048576,timeo=14,soft  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target

  • Systemd .automount unit files: /etc/systemd/system

    mnt-storage_server-Quick_Storage.automount
    [Unit]  
Description=Automount for ZimaOS NAS Quick-Storage  

[Automount]  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/Quick_Storage  
# Unmount if idle for 10 minutes (optional, saves resources)  
TimeoutIdleSec=600  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target
    mnt-storage_server-NVMe.automount
    [Unit]  
Description=Automount for ZimaOS NVMe drive  

[Automount]  
Where=/mnt/storage_server/NVMe  
# Unmount if idle for 10 minutes (optional, saves resources)  
TimeoutIdleSec=600  

[Install]  
WantedBy=multi-user.target

  • Systemd daemon reload command:

    sudo systemd daemon-reload

docs.title: Beszel Agent
docs.text: GitHub Documentation

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Purpose: Agent for Beszel Hub, a server utilization monitor. Allows for client servers to connect to hub.
  • Port(s): 45867
  • URL / Access: N/A
  • Credentials:
    •  Docker Compose: docker-compose.yml

docs.title: Deployment Details
docs.text:
• Method:  Docker Compose
  • Container Name: beszel-agent
  • Host Device:
    • Raspberry Pi 4B Server
      • Image: henrygd/beszel-agent
    • ZimaBoard 2 NAS
      • Image: henrygd/beszel-agent:alpine

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
docker-compose.yml
services:  
  beszel-agent:  
    image: henrygd/beszel-agent  
    container_name: beszel-agent  
    restart: unless-stopped  
    network_mode: host  
    volumes:  
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro  
      - ./beszel_agent_data:/var/lib/beszel-agent  
      # monitor other disks / partitions by mounting a folder in /extra-filesystems  
      # - /mnt/disk/.beszel:/extra-filesystems/sda1:ro  
    environment:  
      LISTEN: 45876  
      KEY: 'ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIK/q0pQSR0NXnA93R74Vdmv05DMmk+OWX36W1KFK31EI'  
      TOKEN: 3630d-aee6185c6-f48d6-f03c1cf38  
      HUB_URL: http://pi-server.internal:8090
docker-compose.yml
name: beszel-agent
services:
  beszel-agent:
    cap_add:
      - SYS_RAWIO
      - SYS_ADMIN
    cpu_shares: 90
    command: []
    container_name: beszel-agent
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 16508309504
        reservations:
          devices: []
    devices:
      - /dev/sda:/dev/sda
      - /dev/sdb:/dev/sdb
      - /dev/nvme0:/dev/nvme0
    environment:
      - HUB_URL=http://pi-server.internal:8090
      - KEY=ssh-ed25519
        AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIK/q0pQSR0NXnA93R74Vdmv05DMmk+OWX36W1KFK31EI
      - LISTEN=45876
      - TOKEN=312a40-12e3243-1faf72-8199904e
    image: henrygd/beszel-agent:alpine
    labels:
      icon: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/selfhst/icons@main/svg/beszel.svg
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - type: bind
        source: /var/run/docker.sock
        target: /var/run/docker.sock
      - type: bind
        source: /media/nvme0n1p1/AppData/beszel_agent_data
        target: /var/lib/beszel-agent
      - type: bind
        source: /media/Quick-Storage/.beszel
        target: /extra-filesystems/md0__Quick_Storage
    ports: []
    network_mode: host
    privileged: false
x-casaos:
  author: self
  category: self
  hostname: ""
  icon: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/selfhst/icons@main/svg/beszel.svg
  index: /
  is_uncontrolled: false
  port_map: "45876"
  scheme: http
  store_app_id: beszel-agent
  title:
    custom: ""
    en_us: beszel-agent

docs.title: Deployment Details
docs.text:
• Host Device: Raspberry Pi 4B Server
  • Method:  Docker Compose
  • Container Name: beszel
  • Image: henrygd/beszel:latest

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
docker-compose.yml
services:
  beszel:
    image: henrygd/beszel:latest
    container_name: beszel
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - 8090:8090
    volumes:
      - ./beszel_data:/beszel_data
      - ./beszel_socket:/beszel_socket
    dns:
      - 192.168.50.6
      - 192.168.50.2

  beszel-agent:
    image: henrygd/beszel-agent
    container_name: beszel-agent
    restart: unless-stopped
    network_mode: host
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
      - ./beszel_agent_data:/var/lib/beszel-agent
      # monitor other disks / partitions by mounting a folder in /extra-filesystems
      # - /mnt/disk/.beszel:/extra-filesystems/sda1:ro
      - /mnt/usb-drive/.beszel:/extra-filesystems/sda1__usb-drive:ro
    environment:
      LISTEN: /beszel_socket/beszel.sock
      KEY: 'ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIK/q0pQSR0NXnA93R74Vdmv05DMmk+OWX36W1KFK31EI'
      TOKEN: 879-279c30f663-5ff-f7ca42f129c
      HUB_URL: http://pi-server.internal:8090
    dns:
      - 192.168.50.6
      - 192.168.50.2

    • 443

docs.title: Deployment Details
docs.text:
• Host Device: Raspberry Pi Zero Server
  • Method:  Native Install (Systemd service)
  • Container Name: N/A
  • Image: N/A

docs.title: Install:
docs.text:
1. Add the official Caddy repository. 

    sudo apt install -y debian-keyring debian-archive-keyring apt-transport-https curl

curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/gpg.key' | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/caddy-stable-archive-keyring.gpg

curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/debian.deb.txt' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/caddy-stable.list

  2. Install the package.

    sudo apt update
sudo apt install caddy

  3. Edit the configuration.

    sudo nano /etc/caddy/Caddyfile

  4. Apply configuration changes by reloading the Systemd service.

    sudo systemctl reload caddy

x-casaos:
  architectures:
    - amd64
    - arm
    - arm64
  author: WisdomSky
  category: Developer
  description:
    de_DE: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel bietet eine einfache Möglichkeit, Webserver sicher im
      Internet bereitzustellen, ohne Firewall-Ports zu öffnen und ACLs zu
      konfigurieren. Cloudflare Tunnel stellt außerdem sicher, dass Anfragen
      über Cloudflare geleitet werden, bevor sie den Webserver erreichen, sodass
      Sie sicher sein können, dass Angriffsverkehr durch Cloudflares WAF und
      unbegrenzte DDoS-Abwehr gestoppt und mit Access authentifiziert wird, wenn
      Sie diese Funktionen für Ihr Konto aktiviert haben.


      Die Software bietet eine nahtlose Möglichkeit, Webserver sicher im Internet bereitzustellen, ohne Firewall-Ports oder Zugriffskontrolllisten (ACLs) zu konfigurieren. Alle Anfragen werden über Cloudflare geleitet, bevor sie Ihren Webserver erreichen, und nutzen Cloudflares Web Application Firewall (WAF) und unbegrenzte DDoS-Abwehr, um Angriffsverkehr zu blockieren, mit optionaler Authentifizierung über Cloudflare Access, falls aktiviert. Mit seiner intuitiven Web-Oberfläche und effizientem Tunnel-Management ist dieses Tool die perfekte Lösung für die sichere Bereitstellung von Webdiensten.


      **Entdecken Sie, wie Sie ZimaOS mit Cloudflare Tunnel verbinden**

      Die Integration von ZimaOS mit Cloudflare Tunnel ermöglicht es Ihnen, lokale Dienste sicher im Internet bereitzustellen, ohne Firewall-Ports zu öffnen, und ermöglicht so einen nahtlosen Fernzugriff. Nachfolgend finden Sie zwei praktische Ressourcen, die Sie durch den Einrichtungsprozess führen:

      1. [**Offizielles Cloudflare-Tutorial**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        Dieses Tutorial bietet detaillierte Schritte zum Erstellen und Verwalten eines Cloudflare-Tunnels.
      2. [**Praktischer Leitfaden von Phiptech**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        Dieser Leitfaden bietet eine präzise, schrittweise Anleitung zum Einrichten von Cloudflare Tunnel auf lokalen Geräten wie ZimaOS, mit praktischen Beispielen, die Benutzern helfen, Dienste einfach im öffentlichen Internet bereitzustellen.
    el_GR: >
      Το Cloudflare Tunnel προσφέρει έναν εύκολο τρόπο να εκθέσετε τους web
      servers σας με ασφάλεια στο διαδίκτυο, χωρίς να ανοίξετε θύρες τείχους
      προστασίας ή να ρυθμίσετε ACLs. Το Cloudflare Tunnel εξασφαλίζει επίσης
      ότι τα αιτήματα δρομολογούνται μέσω του Cloudflare πριν φτάσουν στον web
      server, ώστε να είστε σίγουροι ότι η κυκλοφορία επιθέσεων σταματάει με το
      WAF του Cloudflare και την απεριόριστη μείωση DDoS, και πιστοποιείται με
      το Access εάν έχετε ενεργοποιήσει αυτές τις λειτουργίες για τον λογαριασμό
      σας.


      Το λογισμικό παρέχει έναν απρόσκοπτο τρόπο να εκθέσετε τους web servers σας με ασφάλεια στο διαδίκτυο χωρίς να ρυθμίσετε θύρες τείχους προστασίας ή λίστες ελέγχου πρόσβασης (ACLs). Όλα τα αιτήματα δρομολογούνται μέσω του Cloudflare πριν φτάσουν στον web server σας, αξιοποιώντας το Web Application Firewall (WAF) του Cloudflare και την απεριόριστη μείωση DDoS για να μπλοκάρουν την κυκλοφορία επιθέσεων, με προαιρετική πιστοποίηση μέσω του Cloudflare Access εάν είναι ενεργοποιημένη. Με τη διαισθητική του διεπαφή Web και την αποτελεσματική διαχείριση tunnel, αυτό το εργαλείο είναι η τέλεια λύση για την ασφαλή ανάπτυξη υπηρεσιών web.


      **Ανακαλύψτε πώς να συνδέσετε το ZimaOS στο Cloudflare Tunnel**

      Η ενσωμάτωση του ZimaOS με το Cloudflare Tunnel σας επιτρέπει να εκθέσετε τοπικές υπηρεσίες με ασφάλεια στο διαδίκτυο χωρίς να ανοίξετε θύρες τείχους προστασίας, επιτρέποντας την απρόσκοπτη απομακρυσμένη πρόσβαση. Παρακάτω υπάρχουν δύο πρακτικοί πόροι για να σας καθοδηγήσουν στη διαδικασία ρύθμισης:

      1. [**Επίσημο σεμινάριο Cloudflare**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        Αυτό το σεμινάριο παρέχει λεπτομερή βήματα για τη δημιουργία και τη διαχείριση ενός Cloudflare Tunnel.
      2. [**Πρακτικός οδηγός Phiptech**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        Αυτός ο οδηγός προσφέρει μια συνοπτική, βήμα-βήμα περιήγηση για τη ρύθμιση του Cloudflare Tunnel σε τοπικές συσκευές όπως το ZimaOS, με πρακτικά παραδείγματα για να βοηθήσει τους χρήστες να εκθέσουν εύκολα υπηρεσίες στο δημόσιο διαδίκτυο.
    en_GB: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel offers an easy way to expose web servers securely to the
      internet, without opening up firewall ports and configuring ACLs.
      Cloudflare Tunnel also ensures requests route through Cloudflare before
      reaching the web server, so you can be sure attack traffic is stopped with
      Cloudflare’s WAF and Unmetered DDoS mitigation, and authenticated with
      Access if you’ve enabled those features for your account.


      The software provides a seamless way to securely expose web servers to the internet without configuring firewall ports or access control lists (ACLs). All requests are routed through Cloudflare before reaching your web server, leveraging Cloudflare’s Web Application Firewall (WAF) and unmetered DDoS mitigation to block attack traffic, with optional authentication via Cloudflare Access if enabled. With its intuitive Web interface and efficient tunnel management, this tool is the perfect solution for securely deploying web services.


      **Discover How to Connect ZimaOS to Cloudflare Tunnel**

      Integrating ZimaOS with Cloudflare Tunnel allows you to securely expose local services to the internet without opening firewall ports, enabling seamless remote access. Below are two practical resources to guide you through the setup process:

      1. [**Cloudflare Official Tutorial**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        This tutorial provides detailed steps for creating and managing a Cloudflare Tunnel.
      2. [**Phiptech Practical Guide**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        This guide offers a concise, step-by-step walkthrough for setting up Cloudflare Tunnel on local devices like ZimaOS, with practical examples to help users easily expose services to the public internet.

    fr_FR: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel offre un moyen facile d'exposer des serveurs web de
      manière sécurisée sur Internet, sans ouvrir de ports de pare-feu ni
      configurer d'ACL. Cloudflare Tunnel garantit également que les requêtes
      sont acheminées via Cloudflare avant d'atteindre le serveur web, vous
      pouvez donc être sûr que le trafic d'attaque est arrêté par le WAF de
      Cloudflare et la mitigation DDoS illimitée, et authentifié avec Access si
      vous avez activé ces fonctionnalités pour votre compte.


      Le logiciel fournit un moyen transparent d'exposer de manière sécurisée des serveurs web sur Internet sans configurer de ports de pare-feu ou de listes de contrôle d'accès (ACL). Toutes les requêtes sont acheminées via Cloudflare avant d'atteindre votre serveur web, en tirant parti du pare-feu d'applications web (WAF) de Cloudflare et de la mitigation DDoS illimitée pour bloquer le trafic d'attaque, avec une authentification optionnelle via Cloudflare Access si elle est activée. Avec son interface Web intuitive et sa gestion efficace des tunnels, cet outil est la solution parfaite pour déployer des services web en toute sécurité.


      **Découvrez comment connecter ZimaOS à Cloudflare Tunnel**

      L'intégration de ZimaOS avec Cloudflare Tunnel vous permet d'exposer de manière sécurisée des services locaux sur Internet sans ouvrir de ports de pare-feu, permettant ainsi un accès à distance transparent. Voici deux ressources pratiques pour vous guider tout au long du processus de configuration :

      1. [**Tutoriel officiel de Cloudflare** ](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        Ce tutoriel fournit des étapes détaillées pour créer et gérer un tunnel Cloudflare.
      2. [**Guide pratique de Phiptech** ](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        Ce guide propose une procédure pas à pas concise pour configurer Cloudflare Tunnel sur des appareils locaux comme ZimaOS, avec des exemples pratiques pour aider les utilisateurs à exposer facilement des services sur Internet public.
    hr_HR: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel nudi jednostavan način za sigurno izlaganje web
      poslužitelja internetu, bez otvaranja portova vatrozida i konfiguriranja
      ACL-ova. Cloudflare Tunnel također osigurava da se zahtjevi usmjeravaju
      kroz Cloudflare prije nego što stignu do web poslužitelja, tako da možete
      biti sigurni da se napadački promet zaustavlja pomoću Cloudflareovog WAF-a
      i neograničene DDoS mitigacije, te autentificira pomoću Accessa ako ste
      omogućili te značajke za svoj račun.


      Softver pruža besprijekoran način za sigurno izlaganje web poslužitelja internetu bez konfiguriranja portova vatrozida ili lista kontrole pristupa (ACL-ova). Svi zahtjevi se usmjeravaju kroz Cloudflare prije nego što stignu do vašeg web poslužitelja, koristeći Cloudflareov Web Application Firewall (WAF) i neograničenu DDoS mitigaciju za blokiranje napadačkog prometa, s opcionalnom autentifikacijom putem Cloudflare Accessa ako je omogućena. Sa svojim intuitivnim web sučeljem i učinkovitim upravljanjem tunelima, ovaj alat je savršeno rješenje za sigurno implementiranje web usluga.


      **Otkrijte kako povezati ZimaOS s Cloudflare Tunnelom**

      Integracija ZimaOS-a s Cloudflare Tunnelom omogućuje vam sigurno izlaganje lokalnih usluga internetu bez otvaranja portova vatrozida, omogućujući besprijekoran daljinski pristup. U nastavku su dva praktična resursa koji će vas voditi kroz proces postavljanja:

      1. [**Cloudflare službeni vodič**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        Ovaj vodič pruža detaljne korake za stvaranje i upravljanje Cloudflare Tunnelom.
      2. [**Phiptech praktični vodič**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        Ovaj vodič nudi sažet, korak-po-korak pregled za postavljanje Cloudflare Tunela na lokalnim uređajima poput ZimaOS-a, s praktičnim primjerima koji pomažu korisnicima da lako izlože usluge javnom internetu.
    it_IT: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel offre un modo semplice per esporre i server web in modo
      sicuro su internet, senza aprire porte firewall e configurare ACL.
      Cloudflare Tunnel assicura anche che le richieste passino attraverso
      Cloudflare prima di raggiungere il server web, così puoi essere sicuro che
      il traffico di attacco viene fermato con il WAF di Cloudflare e la
      mitigazione DDoS illimitata, e autenticato con Access se hai abilitato
      queste funzionalità per il tuo account.


      Il software fornisce un modo senza soluzione di continuità per esporre in modo sicuro i server web su internet senza configurare porte firewall o liste di controllo accessi (ACL). Tutte le richieste sono instradate attraverso Cloudflare prima di raggiungere il tuo server web, sfruttando il Web Application Firewall (WAF) di Cloudflare e la mitigazione DDoS illimitata per bloccare il traffico di attacco, con autenticazione opzionale tramite Cloudflare Access se abilitata. Con la sua interfaccia Web intuitiva e la gestione efficiente dei tunnel, questo strumento è la soluzione perfetta per distribuire servizi web in modo sicuro.


      **Scopri come connettere ZimaOS a Cloudflare Tunnel**

      Integrare ZimaOS con Cloudflare Tunnel ti consente di esporre in modo sicuro i servizi locali su internet senza aprire porte firewall, consentendo un accesso remoto senza interruzioni. Di seguito sono riportate due risorse pratiche per guidarti attraverso il processo di configurazione:

      1. [**Tutorial ufficiale di Cloudflare**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        Questo tutorial fornisce passaggi dettagliati per creare e gestire un Tunnel Cloudflare.
      2. [**Guida pratica di Phiptech**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        Questa guida offre una procedura passo-passo concisa per configurare Cloudflare Tunnel su dispositivi locali come ZimaOS, con esempi pratici per aiutare gli utenti a esporre facilmente i servizi su internet pubblico.
    ja_JP: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel は、ファイアウォールポートを開放したり ACL を設定したりせずに、Web
      サーバーを安全にインターネットに公開する簡単な方法を提供します。Cloudflare Tunnel は、リクエストが Web サーバーに到達する前に
      Cloudflare を経由するようにすることで、攻撃トラフィックが Cloudflare の WAF および無制限の DDoS
      緩和によって停止され、アカウントで有効にしている場合は Access で認証されることを保証します。


      このソフトウェアは、ファイアウォールポートやアクセス制御リスト (ACL) を設定せずに、Web サーバーを安全にインターネットに公開するシームレスな方法を提供します。すべてのリクエストは、Web サーバーに到達する前に Cloudflare を経由し、Cloudflare の Web アプリケーション ファイアウォール (WAF) および無制限の DDoS 緩和を活用して攻撃トラフィックをブロックし、有効にしている場合は Cloudflare Access 経由で認証するオプションもあります。直感的な Web インターフェイスと効率的なトンネル管理により、このツールは Web サービスを安全に展開するための完璧なソリューションです。


      **ZimaOS を Cloudflare Tunnel に接続する方法を発見する**

      ZimaOS を Cloudflare Tunnel と統合することで、ファイアウォールポートを開放せずにローカルサービスを安全にインターネットに公開し、シームレスなリモートアクセスを可能にします。以下は、設定プロセスをガイドする 2 つの実用的なリソースです:

      1. [**Cloudflare 公式チュートリアル**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        このチュートリアルでは、Cloudflare Tunnel を作成および管理するための詳細な手順が提供されています。
      2. [**Phiptech 実践ガイド**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        このガイドでは、ZimaOS などのローカルデバイスに Cloudflare Tunnel を設定するための簡潔なステップバイステップのウォークスルーが提供され、ユーザーがサービスをパブリックインターネットに簡単に公開できるよう実用的な例が示されています。
    ko_KR: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel은 방화벽 포트를 열거나 ACL을 구성하지 않고도 웹 서버를 안전하게 인터넷에 노출할 수 있는 쉬운
      방법을 제공합니다. Cloudflare Tunnel은 또한 요청이 웹 서버에 도달하기 전에 Cloudflare를 통해 라우팅되도록
      하여 Cloudflare의 WAF 및 무제한 DDoS 완화 기능으로 공격 트래픽이 차단되고, 계정에 대해 해당 기능을 활성화한 경우
      Access를 통해 인증되도록 합니다.


      이 소프트웨어는 방화벽 포트나 액세스 제어 목록(ACL)을 구성하지 않고도 웹 서버를 안전하게 인터넷에 노출할 수 있는 원활한 방법을 제공합니다. 모든 요청은 웹 서버에 도달하기 전에 Cloudflare를 통해 라우팅되며, Cloudflare의 웹 애플리케이션 방화벽(WAF) 및 무제한 DDoS 완화 기능을 활용하여 공격 트래픽을 차단하고, 활성화된 경우 Cloudflare Access를 통한 선택적 인증을 제공합니다. 직관적인 웹 인터페이스와 효율적인 터널 관리를 통해 이 도구는 웹 서비스를 안전하게 배포하기 위한 완벽한 솔루션입니다.


      **ZimaOS를 Cloudflare Tunnel에 연결하는 방법을 알아보세요**

      ZimaOS를 Cloudflare Tunnel과 통합하면 방화벽 포트를 열지 않고도 로컬 서비스를 안전하게 인터넷에 노출하여 원활한 원격 액세스를 가능하게 합니다. 다음은 설정 과정을 안내하는 두 가지 실용적인 리소스입니다:

      1. [**Cloudflare 공식 튜토리얼**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        이 튜토리얼은 Cloudflare Tunnel을 만들고 관리하는 자세한 단계를 제공합니다.
      2. [**Phiptech 실용 가이드**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        이 가이드는 ZimaOS와 같은 로컬 장치에 Cloudflare Tunnel을 설정하는 간결한 단계별 안내를 제공하며, 사용자가 서비스를 공용 인터넷에 쉽게 노출할 수 있도록 실용적인 예제를 제공합니다。
    nb_NO: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel tilbyr en enkel måte å eksponere webservere sikkert til
      internett, uten å åpne brannmurporter og konfigurere ACL-er. Cloudflare
      Tunnel sørger også for at forespørsler rutes gjennom Cloudflare før de når
      webserveren, slik at du kan være sikker på at angrepstrafikk stoppes med
      Cloudflares WAF og ubegrenset DDoS-mitigering, og autentiseres med Access
      hvis du har aktivert disse funksjonene for kontoen din.


      Programvaren gir en sømløs måte å eksponere webservere sikkert til internett uten å konfigurere brannmurporter eller tilgangskontrollister (ACL-er). Alle forespørsler rutes gjennom Cloudflare før de når webserveren din, og utnytter Cloudflares Web Application Firewall (WAF) og ubegrenset DDoS-mitigering for å blokkere angrepstrafikk, med valgfri autentisering via Cloudflare Access hvis aktivert. Med sitt intuitive webgrensesnitt og effektiv tunneladministrasjon er dette verktøyet den perfekte løsningen for sikker distribusjon av webtjenester.


      **Oppdag hvordan du kobler ZimaOS til Cloudflare Tunnel**

      Å integrere ZimaOS med Cloudflare Tunnel lar deg eksponere lokale tjenester sikkert til internett uten å åpne brannmurporter, noe som muliggjør sømløs fjernaksess. Nedenfor er to praktiske ressurser for å veilede deg gjennom oppsettprosessen:

      1. [**Cloudflare offisiell veiledning**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        Denne veiledningen gir detaljerte trinn for å opprette og administrere en Cloudflare Tunnel.
      2. [**Phiptech praktisk guide**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        Denne guiden tilbyr en kortfattet, trinn-for-trinn-gjennomgang for å sette opp Cloudflare Tunnel på lokale enheter som ZimaOS, med praktiske eksempler for å hjelpe brukere med å eksponere tjenester til det offentlige internett.
    pt_PT: >
      O Cloudflare Tunnel oferece uma forma fácil de expor servidores web de
      forma segura à internet, sem abrir portas de firewall e configurar ACLs. O
      Cloudflare Tunnel também garante que os pedidos são encaminhados através
      do Cloudflare antes de chegarem ao servidor web, para que possa ter a
      certeza de que o tráfego de ataque é interrompido com o WAF do Cloudflare
      e a mitigação DDoS ilimitada, e autenticado com o Access se tiver ativado
      essas funcionalidades para a sua conta.


      O software fornece uma forma transparente de expor servidores web de forma segura à internet sem configurar portas de firewall ou listas de controlo de acesso (ACLs). Todos os pedidos são encaminhados através do Cloudflare antes de chegarem ao seu servidor web, aproveitando o Web Application Firewall (WAF) do Cloudflare e a mitigação DDoS ilimitada para bloquear o tráfego de ataque, com autenticação opcional através do Cloudflare Access se ativada. Com a sua interface Web intuitiva e gestão eficiente de túneis, esta ferramenta é a solução perfeita para implementar serviços web de forma segura.


      **Descubra como ligar o ZimaOS ao Cloudflare Tunnel**

      Integrar o ZimaOS com o Cloudflare Tunnel permite-lhe expor serviços locais de forma segura à internet sem abrir portas de firewall, permitindo um acesso remoto transparente. Abaixo estão dois recursos práticos para o guiar através do processo de configuração:

      1. [**Tutorial oficial do Cloudflare**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        Este tutorial fornece passos detalhados para criar e gerir um Cloudflare Tunnel.
      2. [**Guia prático da Phiptech**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        Este guia oferece um passo a passo conciso para configurar o Cloudflare Tunnel em dispositivos locais como o ZimaOS, com exemplos práticos para ajudar os utilizadores a expor facilmente serviços à internet pública.
    ru_RU: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel предлагает простой способ безопасного подключения
      веб-серверов к Интернету, не открывая порты брандмауэра и не настраивая
      ACL. Cloudflare Tunnel также обеспечивает маршрутизацию запросов через
      Cloudflare до того, как они достигнут веб-сервера, поэтому вы можете быть
      уверены, что трафик атак блокируется с помощью WAF Cloudflare и
      неограниченной защиты от DDoS, а также аутентифицируется с помощью Access,
      если вы включили эти функции для своей учетной записи.


      Программное обеспечение обеспечивает беспрепятственный способ безопасного подключения веб-серверов к Интернету без настройки портов брандмауэра или списков контроля доступа (ACL). Все запросы направляются через Cloudflare до того, как они достигнут вашего веб-сервера, используя Web Application Firewall (WAF) Cloudflare и неограниченную защиту от DDoS для блокировки трафика атак, с возможностью аутентификации через Cloudflare Access, если она включена. Благодаря интуитивно понятному веб-интерфейсу и эффективному управлению туннелями этот инструмент является идеальным решением для безопасного развертывания веб-служб.


      **Узнайте, как подключить ZimaOS к Cloudflare Tunnel**

      Интеграция ZimaOS с Cloudflare Tunnel позволяет безопасно подключать локальные службы к Интернету, не открывая порты брандмауэра, что обеспечивает беспрепятственный удаленный доступ. Ниже приведены два практических ресурса, которые помогут вам в процессе настройки:

      1. [**Официальное руководство Cloudflare**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        Это руководство предоставляет подробные шаги по созданию и управлению Cloudflare Tunnel.
      2. [**Практическое руководство Phiptech**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        Это руководство предлагает краткое пошаговое руководство по настройке Cloudflare Tunnel на локальных устройствах, таких как ZimaOS, с практическими примерами, помогающими пользователям легко подключать службы к общедоступному Интернету.
    sv_SE: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel erbjuder ett enkelt sätt att exponera webbservrar säkert
      till internet, utan att öppna brandväggsportar och konfigurera ACL:er.
      Cloudflare Tunnel ser också till att förfrågningar dirigeras genom
      Cloudflare innan de når webbservrarna, så du kan vara säker på att
      attacktrafik stoppas med Cloudflares WAF och obegränsad DDoS-mitigering,
      och autentiseras med Access om du har aktiverat dessa funktioner för ditt
      konto.


      Mjukvaran tillhandahåller ett sömlöst sätt att säkert exponera webbservrar till internet utan att konfigurera brandväggsportar eller åtkomstkontrollistor (ACL:er). Alla förfrågningar dirigeras genom Cloudflare innan de når din webbservrar, och utnyttjar Cloudflares Web Application Firewall (WAF) och obegränsad DDoS-mitigering för att blockera attacktrafik, med valfri autentisering via Cloudflare Access om det är aktiverat. Med sitt intuitiva webbgränssnitt och effektiv tunnelhantering är detta verktyg den perfekta lösningen för att säkert distribuera webbtjänster.


      **Upptäck hur du ansluter ZimaOS till Cloudflare Tunnel**

      Att integrera ZimaOS med Cloudflare Tunnel låter dig säkert exponera lokala tjänster till internet utan att öppna brandväggsportar, vilket möjliggör sömlös fjärråtkomst. Nedan finns två praktiska resurser för att guida dig genom installationsprocessen:

      1. [**Cloudflares officiella handledning**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        Denna handledning ger detaljerade steg för att skapa och hantera en Cloudflare Tunnel.
      2. [**Phiptechs praktiska guide**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        Denna guide erbjuder en kortfattad, steg-för-steg-genomgång för att konfigurera Cloudflare Tunnel på lokala enheter som ZimaOS, med praktiska exempel för att hjälpa användare att enkelt exponera tjänster till det offentliga internet.
    tr_TR: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel, web sunucularını güvenli bir şekilde internete maruz
      bırakmanın kolay bir yolunu sunar, güvenlik duvarı portlarını açmadan ve
      ACL'leri yapılandırmadan. Cloudflare Tunnel ayrıca isteklerin web
      sunucusuna ulaşmadan önce Cloudflare üzerinden yönlendirilmesini sağlar,
      böylece saldırı trafiğinin Cloudflare'ın WAF ve sınırsız DDoS azaltma ile
      durdurulduğundan ve hesabınız için bu özellikleri etkinleştirdiyseniz
      Access ile kimlik doğrulandığından emin olabilirsiniz.


      Yazılım, güvenlik duvarı portlarını veya erişim kontrol listelerini (ACL'ler) yapılandırmadan web sunucularını güvenli bir şekilde internete maruz bırakmanın sorunsuz bir yolunu sunar. Tüm istekler, web sunucunuza ulaşmadan önce Cloudflare üzerinden yönlendirilir, Cloudflare'ın Web Uygulama Güvenlik Duvarı (WAF) ve sınırsız DDoS azaltma özelliklerini kullanarak saldırı trafiğini engeller, etkinleştirilmişse Cloudflare Access aracılığıyla isteğe bağlı kimlik doğrulama ile. Sezgisel Web arayüzü ve verimli tünel yönetimi ile bu araç, web hizmetlerini güvenli bir şekilde dağıtmak için mükemmel bir çözümdür.


      **ZimaOS'u Cloudflare Tunnel'a Nasıl Bağlayacağınızı Keşfedin**

      ZimaOS'u Cloudflare Tunnel ile entegre etmek, güvenlik duvarı portlarını açmadan yerel hizmetleri güvenli bir şekilde internete maruz bırakmanıza olanak tanır, sorunsuz uzaktan erişim sağlar. Aşağıda, kurulum sürecinde size rehberlik edecek iki pratik kaynak bulunmaktadır:

      1. [**Cloudflare Resmi Eğitimi**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/): 
        Bu eğitim, Cloudflare Tunnel oluşturma ve yönetme için ayrıntılı adımlar sağlar.
      2. [**Phiptech Pratik Kılavuzu**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/): 
        Bu kılavuz, ZimaOS gibi yerel cihazlarda Cloudflare Tunnel kurulumunu adım adım anlatan kısa bir rehber sunar, kullanıcıların hizmetleri genel internete kolayca maruz bırakmalarına yardımcı olacak pratik örneklerle.
    zh_CN: >
      Cloudflare Tunnel 提供了一种简单的方法，可以安全地将 Web 服务器暴露到互联网上，而无需打开防火墙端口和配置
      ACL。Cloudflare Tunnel 还确保请求在到达 Web 服务器之前通过 Cloudflare 路由，因此您可以确信攻击流量会被
      Cloudflare 的 WAF 和无限制 DDoS 缓解措施阻止，如果您为帐户启用了这些功能，还会通过 Access 进行身份验证。


      该软件提供了一种无缝的方式，可以安全地将 Web 服务器暴露到互联网上，而无需配置防火墙端口或访问控制列表 (ACL)。所有请求在到达您的 Web 服务器之前都会通过 Cloudflare 路由，利用 Cloudflare 的 Web 应用程序防火墙 (WAF) 和无限制 DDoS 缓解措施来阻止攻击流量，如果启用了 Cloudflare Access，还可以选择通过其进行身份验证。凭借其直观的 Web 界面和高效的隧道管理，此工具是安全部署 Web 服务的完美解决方案。


      **探索如何将 ZimaOS 连接到 Cloudflare Tunnel**

      将 ZimaOS 与 Cloudflare Tunnel 集成，允许您安全地将本地服务暴露到互联网上，而无需打开防火墙端口，从而实现无缝的远程访问。以下是两个实用的资源，可指导您完成设置过程：

      1. [**Cloudflare 官方教程**](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/get-started/create-remote-tunnel/)：
        本教程提供了创建和管理 Cloudflare Tunnel 的详细步骤。
      2. [**Phiptech 实用指南**](https://phiptech.com/how-to-setup-cloudflare-tunnel-and-expose-your-local-service-or-application/)：
        本指南提供了在 ZimaOS 等本地设备上设置 Cloudflare Tunnel 的简洁分步演练，并附有实用示例，帮助用户轻松将服务暴露到公共互联网。

docs.title: Dockge
docs.text: GitHub Documentation

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Purpose: A fancy, easy-to-use and reactive self-hosted Docker compose.yaml stack-oriented manager.
  • Port(s): 5001
  • URL / Access:
    • http://pi-server.internal:5001
    • http://pi-zero.internal:5001
    • http://debian-vm.internal:5001
  • Credentials:
    • Bitwarden Bitwarden:
      • "Dockge @ pi-server"
      • "Dockge @ pi-zero"
      • "Dockge @ debian-vm"

docs.title: Deployment Details
docs.text:
• Host Device:
    • Raspberry Pi 4B Server
    • Raspberry Pi Zero Server
    • Debian Server VM
  • Method:  Docker Compose
  • Container Name: dockge-dockge-1
  • Image: louislam/dockge:1

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
docker-compose.yml
services:
  dockge:
    image: louislam/dockge:1
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      # Host Port : Container Port
      - 5001:5001
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - ./data:/app/data

      # If you want to use private registries, you need to share the auth file with Dockge:
      # - /root/.docker/:/root/.docker

      # Stacks Directory
      # ⚠️ READ IT CAREFULLY. If you did it wrong, your data could end up writing into a WRONG PATH.
      # ⚠️ 1. FULL path only. No relative path (MUST)
      # ⚠️ 2. Left Stacks Path === Right Stacks Path (MUST)
      - /opt/stacks:/opt/stacks
    environment:
      # Tell Dockge where is your stacks directory
      - DOCKGE_STACKS_DIR=/opt/stacks
      - DOCKGE_CONSOLE_ENBLE=true

docs.title: F1 Replay Timing
docs.text: GitHub Documentation

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Purpose: Visualization of real-time track data and telemetry synced to F1 live and replays.
  • Port(s):
    • 3000
    • 8000
  • URL / Access:
    • Backend: http://storage-server.internal:8000
    • Frontend: http://storage-server.internal:3000
  • Credentials: N/A

docs.title: Deployment Details
docs.text:
• Host Device:
    • ZimaBoard 2 NAS
  • Method:  Docker Compose (Stack)
  • Container Names:
    • f1-backend
    • f1-frontend
  • Images:
    • ghcr.io/adn8naiagent/f1replaytiming-backend:latest
    • ghcr.io/adn8naiagent/f1replaytiming-frontend:latest

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
docker-compose.yml
services:
  backend:
    image: ghcr.io/adn8naiagent/f1replaytiming-backend:latest
    container_name: f1-backend
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      - FRONTEND_URL=http://storage-server.internal:3000 # (1)!
      - DATA_DIR=/data
    volumes:
      - /media/nvme0n1p1/AppData/f1-replay-timing/f1data:/data
      - /media/nvme0n1p1/AppData/f1-replay-timing/f1cache:/data/fastf1-cache
    networks:
      - host

  frontend:
    image: ghcr.io/adn8naiagent/f1replaytiming-frontend:latest
    container_name: f1_frontend
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://storage-server.internal:8000 # (2)!
    depends_on:
      - backend
    networks:
      - host

networks:
  - host

volumes:
  f1data:
  f1cache:

  1. Do NOT use 127.0.0.1 or localhost. The application running in the client Web browser will think the backend / frontend is being hosted on the local machine. Use the IP address or URL of the host server.

  2. Do NOT use 127.0.0.1 or localhost. The application running in the client Web browser will think the backend / frontend is being hosted on the local machine. Use the IP address or URL of the host server.

docs.title: Install:
docs.text:
Setup Creality Helper Script
# Enter the following command to download the Creality-Helper-Script to the `/usr/data/helper-script` directory.
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Guilouz/Creality-Helper-Script.git /usr/data/helper-script

# Then enter the following command to run the helper-script:
sh /usr/data/helper-script/helper.sh

# If you encounter an issue to clone Helper Script repository, enter this command before cloning:
git config --global http.sslVerify false
